steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what point the text makes specifically about *gogogs*. The answer must be directly stated in the passage, not inferred.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "It contains a repetition of the element "go" in *gogs*.": The passage states explicitly: "the element 'go' in *gogs* gets repeated in *gogogs*." This is a direct match. ✓
  • "It doesn't have a clear equivalent in English.": The passage never mentions English or any comparison to English. Not stated. ✗
  • The remaining alternative uses reduplication.": The passage says the opposite — "there are many examples of this type of reduplication in O'odham." ✗
  • "It is identical in meaning to several other words in O'odham.": *Gogogs* means "several dogs" — it is related to *gogs* ("a dog"), but the passage never claims it is identical in meaning to other O'odham words. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
